authors_text: Yassine Naji,Aleksandr Setkov,Angélique Loesch,Michèle Gouiffès,Romaric Audigier
title: Spatio-temporal predictive tasks for abnormal event detection in videos
title_zh: 用于视频中异常事件检测的时空预测任务
comment: Accepted at the 18th IEEE International Conference on Advanced Video and Signal Based Surveillance (AVSS), 2022
comments: Accepted at the 18th IEEE International Conference on Advanced Video and Signal Based Surveillance (AVSS), 2022
abstract: Abnormal event detection in videos is a challenging problem, partly due to the multiplicity of abnormal patterns and the lack of their corresponding annotations. In this paper, we propose new constrained pretext tasks to learn object level normality patterns. Our approach consists in learning a mapping between down-scaled visual queries and their corresponding normal appearance and motion characteristics at the original resolution. The proposed tasks are more challenging than reconstruction and future frame prediction tasks which are widely used in the literature, since our model learns to jointly predict spatial and temporal features rather than reconstructing them. We believe that more constrained pretext tasks induce a better learning of normality patterns. Experiments on several benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach to localize and track anomalies as it outperforms or reaches the current state-of-the-art on spatio-temporal evaluation metrics.
summary: Abnormal event detection in videos is a challenging problem, partly due to the multiplicity of abnormal patterns and the lack of their corresponding annotations. In this paper, we propose new constrained pretext tasks to learn object level normality patterns. Our approach consists in learning a mapping between down-scaled visual queries and their corresponding normal appearance and motion characteristics at the original resolution. The proposed tasks are more challenging than reconstruction and future frame prediction tasks which are widely used in the literature, since our model learns to jointly predict spatial and temporal features rather than reconstructing them. We believe that more constrained pretext tasks induce a better learning of normality patterns. Experiments on several benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach to localize and track anomalies as it outperforms or reaches the current state-of-the-art on spatio-temporal evaluation metrics.
abstract_zh: 视频中的异常事件检测是一个具有挑战性的问题，部分原因是异常模式的多样性以及缺乏相应的注释。在本文中，我们提出了新的约束借口任务来学习对象级正态模式。我们的方法包括学习缩小的视觉查询与其在原始分辨率下相应的正常外观和运动特征之间的映射。所提出的任务比文献中广泛使用的重建和未来帧预测任务更具挑战性，因为我们的模型学习联合预测空间和时间特征而不是重建它们。我们相信，更多受限的借口任务可以更好地学习常态模式。对多个基准数据集的实验证明了我们的方法定位和跟踪异常的有效性，因为它在时空评估指标上优于或达到当前最先进的水平。

AI.tldr: 本文提出新的时空预测任务用于视频异常事件检测，通过学习降尺度视觉查询与原始分辨率正常特征的映射关系。
AI.motivation: 视频异常检测因异常模式多样且缺乏标注而困难，现有重建和未来帧预测任务无法有效捕捉时空特征。
AI.method: 设计约束性预训练任务，学习降尺度视觉查询与原始分辨率下的正常外观及运动特征映射，联合预测时空信息。
AI.result: 在多个基准数据集上实现异常定位与跟踪的最优或接近最优性能，优于现有方法。
AI.conclusion: 更约束的预训练任务能更好学习正常模式，提升异常检测效果。

The proposed tasks are more challenging than reconstruction and future frame prediction tasks which are widely used in the literature, since our model learns to jointly predict spatial and temporal features rather than reconstructing them. We believe that more constrained pretext tasks induce a better learning of normality patterns. Experiments on several benchmark datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach to localize and track anomalies as it outperforms or reaches the current state-of-the-art on spatio-temporal evaluation metrics. 视频中的异常事件检测是一个具有挑战性的问题，部分原因是异常模式的多样性以及缺乏相应的注释。在本文中，我们提出了新的约束借口任务来学习对象级正态模式。我们的方法包括学习缩小的视觉查询与其在原始分辨率下相应的正常外观和运动特征之间的映射。所提出的任务比文献中广泛使用的重建和未来帧预测任务更具挑战性，因为我们的模型学习联合预测空间和时间特征而不是重建它们。我们相信，更多受限的借口任务可以更好地学习常态模式。对多个基准数据集的实验证明了我们的方法定位和跟踪异常的有效性，因为它在时空评估指标上优于或达到当前最先进的水平。 本文提出新的时空预测任务用于视频异常事件检测，通过学习降尺度视觉查询与原始分辨率正常特征的映射关系。 Yassine Naji,Aleksandr Setkov,Angélique Loesch,Michèle Gouiffès,Romaric Audigier cs.CV

authors_text: Siddhant Arora,Siddharth Dalmia,Brian Yan,Florian Metze,Alan W Black,Shinji Watanabe
title: Token-level Sequence Labeling for Spoken Language Understanding using Compositional End-to-End Models
title_zh: 使用组合端到端模型进行口语理解的标记级序列标记
comment: Accepted at EMNLP 2022 Findings. Our code and models will be publicly available as part of the ESPnet-SLU toolkit: https://github.com/espnet/espnet and the release can be followed here: https://github.com/espnet/espnet/pull/4735
comments: Accepted at EMNLP 2022 Findings. Our code and models will be publicly available as part of the ESPnet-SLU toolkit: https://github.com/espnet/espnet and the release can be followed here: https://github.com/espnet/espnet/pull/4735
abstract: End-to-end spoken language understanding (SLU) systems are gaining popularity over cascaded approaches due to their simplicity and ability to avoid error propagation. However, these systems model sequence labeling as a sequence prediction task causing a divergence from its well-established token-level tagging formulation. We build compositional end-to-end SLU systems that explicitly separate the added complexity of recognizing spoken mentions in SLU from the NLU task of sequence labeling. By relying on intermediate decoders trained for ASR, our end-to-end systems transform the input modality from speech to token-level representations that can be used in the traditional sequence labeling framework. This composition of ASR and NLU formulations in our end-to-end SLU system offers direct compatibility with pre-trained ASR and NLU systems, allows performance monitoring of individual components and enables the use of globally normalized losses like CRF, making them attractive in practical scenarios. Our models outperform both cascaded and direct end-to-end models on a labeling task of named entity recognition across SLU benchmarks.
summary: End-to-end spoken language understanding (SLU) systems are gaining popularity over cascaded approaches due to their simplicity and ability to avoid error propagation. However, these systems model sequence labeling as a sequence prediction task causing a divergence from its well-established token-level tagging formulation. We build compositional end-to-end SLU systems that explicitly separate the added complexity of recognizing spoken mentions in SLU from the NLU task of sequence labeling. By relying on intermediate decoders trained for ASR, our end-to-end systems transform the input modality from speech to token-level representations that can be used in the traditional sequence labeling framework. This composition of ASR and NLU formulations in our end-to-end SLU system offers direct compatibility with pre-trained ASR and NLU systems, allows performance monitoring of individual components and enables the use of globally normalized losses like CRF, making them attractive in practical scenarios. Our models outperform both cascaded and direct end-to-end models on a labeling task of named entity recognition across SLU benchmarks.
abstract_zh: 端到端口语理解 (SLU) 系统由于其简单性和避免错误传播的能力而比级联方法更受欢迎。然而，这些系统将序列标记建模为序列预测任务，导致与其完善的标记级标记公式存在分歧。我们构建了组合式端到端 SLU 系统，该系统将 SLU 中识别口头提及所增加的复杂性与序列标记的 NLU 任务明确分开。通过依赖针对 ASR 训练的中间解码器，我们的端到端系统将输入模态从语音转换为可在传统序列标记框架中使用的标记级表示。我们的端到端 SLU 系统中的 ASR 和 NLU 公式组合提供了与预先训练的 ASR 和 NLU 系统的直接兼容性，允许对各个组件进行性能监控，并允许使用 CRF 等全局标准化损失，使其在实际场景中具有吸引力。我们的模型在跨 SLU 基准的命名实体识别标记任务上优于级联和直接端到端模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种组合端到端模型，通过分离语音识别（ASR）和自然语言理解（NLU）任务，改进语音语言理解（SLU）中的token级序列标注方法。
AI.motivation: 现有端到端SLU系统将序列标注视为序列预测任务，偏离了传统token级标注框架，而级联方法存在错误传播问题。需设计能兼容预训练模型并提升性能的组合架构。
AI.method: 构建包含中间ASR解码器的端到端SLU系统，将语音转换为token级表示后输入传统序列标注框架，并结合CRF等全局归一化损失进行训练。
AI.result: 在SLU基准测试的命名实体识别任务中，该模型性能优于级联系统和直接端到端模型。
AI.conclusion: 组合架构通过解耦ASR与NLU任务，兼容预训练模型、支持组件级监控及CRF优化，具有实际应用优势。

authors_text: Sachin Chhabra,Prabal Bijoy Dutta,Hemanth Venkateswara,Baoxin Li
title: PatchRot: A Self-Supervised Technique for Training Vision Transformers
title_zh: PatchRot：一种用于训练视觉 Transformer 的自监督技术
comment: NeurIPS Workshop on Vision Transformers: Theory and Applications (VTTA)
comments: NeurIPS Workshop on Vision Transformers: Theory and Applications (VTTA)
abstract: Vision transformers require a huge amount of labeled data to outperform convolutional neural networks. However, labeling a huge dataset is a very expensive process. Self-supervised learning techniques alleviate this problem by learning features similar to supervised learning in an unsupervised way. In this paper, we propose a self-supervised technique PatchRot that is crafted for vision transformers. PatchRot rotates images and image patches and trains the network to predict the rotation angles. The network learns to extract both global and local features from an image. Our extensive experiments on different datasets showcase PatchRot training learns rich features which outperform supervised learning and compared baseline.
summary: Vision transformers require a huge amount of labeled data to outperform convolutional neural networks. However, labeling a huge dataset is a very expensive process. Self-supervised learning techniques alleviate this problem by learning features similar to supervised learning in an unsupervised way. In this paper, we propose a self-supervised technique PatchRot that is crafted for vision transformers. PatchRot rotates images and image patches and trains the network to predict the rotation angles. The network learns to extract both global and local features from an image. Our extensive experiments on different datasets showcase PatchRot training learns rich features which outperform supervised learning and compared baseline.
abstract_zh: 视觉转换器需要大量标记数据才能超越卷积神经网络。然而，标记巨大的数据集是一个非常昂贵的过程。自监督学习技术通过以无监督的方式学习类似于监督学习的特征来缓解这个问题。在本文中，我们提出了一种专为视觉变压器设计的自监督技术 PatchRot。 PatchRot 旋转图像和图像块并训练网络来预测旋转角度。网络学习从图像中提取全局和局部特征。我们对不同数据集的广泛实验展示了 PatchRot 训练学习了丰富的特征，这些特征优于监督学习并比较基线。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种名为PatchRot的自监督技术，用于训练视觉Transformer模型。
AI.motivation: 视觉Transformer需要大量标注数据才能超越卷积神经网络，而标注数据成本高昂，自监督学习可缓解此问题。
AI.method: 通过旋转图像及其图像块，训练网络预测旋转角度，使模型同时学习全局和局部特征。
AI.result: 在多个数据集上的实验表明，PatchRot学习到的特征优于监督学习和现有基线方法。
AI.conclusion: PatchRot是一种有效的自监督预训练方法，为视觉Transformer提供了高质量的特征学习方案。

authors_text: Madeline Navarro,Santiago Segarra
title: GraphMAD: Graph Mixup for Data Augmentation using Data-Driven Convex Clustering
title_zh: GraphMAD：使用数据驱动的凸聚类进行数据增强的图形混合
comment: 5 pages, 2 figures, 2 tables, submitted to ICASSP 2023
comments: 5 pages, 2 figures, 2 tables, submitted to ICASSP 2023
abstract: We develop a novel data-driven nonlinear mixup mechanism for graph data augmentation and present different mixup functions for sample pairs and their labels. Mixup is a data augmentation method to create new training data by linearly interpolating between pairs of data samples and their labels. Mixup of graph data is challenging since the interpolation between graphs of potentially different sizes is an ill-posed operation. Hence, a promising approach for graph mixup is to first project the graphs onto a common latent feature space and then explore linear and nonlinear mixup strategies in this latent space. In this context, we propose to (i) project graphs onto the latent space of continuous random graph models known as graphons, (ii) leverage convex clustering in this latent space to generate nonlinear data-driven mixup functions, and (iii) investigate the use of different mixup functions for labels and data samples. We evaluate our graph data augmentation performance on benchmark datasets and demonstrate that nonlinear data-driven mixup functions can significantly improve graph classification.
summary: We develop a novel data-driven nonlinear mixup mechanism for graph data augmentation and present different mixup functions for sample pairs and their labels. Mixup is a data augmentation method to create new training data by linearly interpolating between pairs of data samples and their labels. Mixup of graph data is challenging since the interpolation between graphs of potentially different sizes is an ill-posed operation. Hence, a promising approach for graph mixup is to first project the graphs onto a common latent feature space and then explore linear and nonlinear mixup strategies in this latent space. In this context, we propose to (i) project graphs onto the latent space of continuous random graph models known as graphons, (ii) leverage convex clustering in this latent space to generate nonlinear data-driven mixup functions, and (iii) investigate the use of different mixup functions for labels and data samples. We evaluate our graph data augmentation performance on benchmark datasets and demonstrate that nonlinear data-driven mixup functions can significantly improve graph classification.
abstract_zh: 我们开发了一种新颖的数据驱动的非线性混合机制，用于图数据增强，并为样本对及其标签提供了不同的混合函数。 Mixup 是一种数据增强方法，通过在数据样本对及其标签之间进行线性插值来创建新的训练数据。图数据的混合具有挑战性，因为可能不同大小的图之间的插值是一种不适定操作。因此，一种有前途的图混合方法是首先将图投影到公共潜在特征空间上，然后探索该潜在空间中的线性和非线性混合策略。在这种情况下，我们建议（i）将图投影到称为图子的连续随机图模型的潜在空间上，（ii）利用该潜在空间中的凸聚类来生成非线性数据驱动的混合函数，以及（iii）研究标签和数据样本的不同混合函数的使用。我们评估了基准数据集上的图数据增强性能，并证明非线性数据驱动的混合函数可以显着改善图分类。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于数据驱动凸聚类的图数据增强方法GraphMAD，通过非线性mixup机制解决图数据插值难题。
AI.motivation: 传统mixup方法难以直接应用于不同规模的图数据，需在潜在空间中实现有效插值以提升图分类性能。
AI.method: 将图投影至图论模型的潜在空间，利用凸聚类生成非线性mixup函数，分别处理数据样本与标签的混合操作。
AI.result: 在基准数据集上验证表明，非线性数据驱动mixup函数显著优于传统方法，提升图分类准确率。
AI.conclusion: 非线性数据驱动的图mixup方法有效解决了图数据增强难题，为图神经网络训练提供新思路。

search_text: GraphMAD: Graph Mixup for Data Augmentation using Data-Driven Convex Clustering GraphMAD：使用数据驱动的凸聚类进行数据增强的图形混合 We develop a novel data-driven nonlinear mixup mechanism for graph data augmentation and present different mixup functions for sample pairs and their labels. Mixup is a data augmentation method to create new training data by linearly interpolating between pairs of data samples and their labels. Mixup of graph data is challenging since the interpolation between graphs of potentially different sizes is an ill-posed operation. Hence, a promising approach for graph mixup is to first project the graphs onto a common latent feature space and then explore linear and nonlinear mixup strategies in this latent space. In this context, we propose to (i) project graphs onto the latent space of continuous random graph models known as graphons, (ii) leverage convex clustering in this latent space to generate nonlinear data-driven mixup functions, and (iii) investigate the use of different mixup functions for labels and data samples. We evaluate our graph data augmentation performance on benchmark datasets and demonstrate that nonlinear data-driven mixup functions can significantly improve graph classification. 我们开发了一种新颖的数据驱动的非线性混合机制，用于图数据增强，并为样本对及其标签提供了不同的混合函数。 Mixup 是一种数据增强方法，通过在数据样本对及其标签之间进行线性插值来创建新的训练数据。图数据的混合具有挑战性，因为可能不同大小的图之间的插值是一种不适定操作。因此，一种有前途的图混合方法是首先将图投影到公共潜在特征空间上，然后探索该潜在空间中的线性和非线性混合策略。在这种情况下，我们建议（i）将图投影到称为图子的连续随机图模型的潜在空间上，（ii）利用该潜在空间中的凸聚类来生成非线性数据驱动的混合函数，以及（iii）研究标签和数据样本的不同混合函数的使用。我们评估了基准数据集上的图数据增强性能，并证明非线性数据驱动的混合函数可以显着改善图分类。 本文提出一种基于数据驱动凸聚类的图数据增强方法GraphMAD，通过非线性mixup机制解决图数据插值难题。 Madeline Navarro,Santiago Segarra cs.LG,cs.AI

authors_text: Rafael Lahoz-Beltra
title: Solving the Schrodinger equation with genetic algorithms: a practical approach
title_zh: 用遗传算法求解薛定谔方程：一种实用方法
abstract: The Schrodinger equation is one of the most important equations in physics and chemistry and can be solved in the simplest cases by computer numerical methods. Since the beginning of the 70s of the last century the computer began to be used to solve this equation in elementary quantum systems, e.g. and in the most complex case a hydrogen-like system. Obtaining the solution means finding the wave function, which allows predicting the physical and chemical properties of the quantum system. However, when a quantum system is more complex than a hydrogen-like system then we must be satisfied with an approximate solution of the equation. During the last decade the application of algorithms and principles of quantum computation in disciplines other than physics and chemistry, such as biology and artificial intelligence, has led to the search for alternative techniques with which to obtain approximate solutions of the Schrodinger equation. In this paper, we review and illustrate the application of genetic algorithms, i.e. stochastic optimization procedures inspired by Darwinian evolution, in elementary quantum systems and in quantum models of artificial intelligence. In this last field, we illustrate with two toy models how to solve the Schrodinger equation in an elementary model of a quantum neuron and in the synthesis of quantum circuits controlling the behavior of a Braitenberg vehicle.
summary: The Schrodinger equation is one of the most important equations in physics and chemistry and can be solved in the simplest cases by computer numerical methods. Since the beginning of the 70s of the last century the computer began to be used to solve this equation in elementary quantum systems, e.g. and in the most complex case a hydrogen-like system. Obtaining the solution means finding the wave function, which allows predicting the physical and chemical properties of the quantum system. However, when a quantum system is more complex than a hydrogen-like system then we must be satisfied with an approximate solution of the equation. During the last decade the application of algorithms and principles of quantum computation in disciplines other than physics and chemistry, such as biology and artificial intelligence, has led to the search for alternative techniques with which to obtain approximate solutions of the Schrodinger equation. In this paper, we review and illustrate the application of genetic algorithms, i.e. stochastic optimization procedures inspired by Darwinian evolution, in elementary quantum systems and in quantum models of artificial intelligence. In this last field, we illustrate with two toy models how to solve the Schrodinger equation in an elementary model of a quantum neuron and in the synthesis of quantum circuits controlling the behavior of a Braitenberg vehicle.
abstract_zh: 薛定谔方程是物理和化学中最重要的方程之一，可以在最简单的情况下通过计算机数值方法求解。自上世纪70年代初以来，计算机开始用于求解基本量子系统中的该方程，例如在最复杂的情​​况下是类氢系统。获得解意味着找到波函数，从而可以预测量子系统的物理和化学性质。然而，当量子系统比类氢系统更复杂时，我们必须满足于方程的近似解。在过去的十年中，量子计算的算法和原理在物理和化学以外的学科（例如生物学和人工智能）中的应用导致人们寻找替代技术来获得薛定谔方程的近似解。在本文中，我们回顾并说明了遗传算法（即受达尔文进化论启发的随机优化程序）在基本量子系统和人工智能量子模型中的应用。在最后一个领域，我们用两个玩具模型说明了如何在量子神经元的基本模型中以及在控制布雷滕伯格车辆行为的量子电路的合成中求解薛定谔方程。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了利用遗传算法求解复杂量子系统中的薛定谔方程，并将其应用于量子神经元模型和量子电路合成中。
AI.motivation: 传统数值方法难以处理复杂量子系统，而遗传算法作为受达尔文进化启发的随机优化方法，为求解薛定谔方程提供了新思路。
AI.method: 采用遗传算法对量子系统进行优化计算，通过两个简化模型（量子神经元和Braitenberg车辆电路）验证方法有效性。
AI.result: 展示了遗传算法在量子神经元模型和量子电路控制中的可行性，为复杂量子系统的近似求解提供了实践方案。
AI.conclusion: 遗传算法可作为传统方法的补充，为复杂量子系统及量子人工智能模型的求解提供有效途径。

authors_text: Muqiao Yang,Naoyuki Kanda,Xiaofei Wang,Jian Wu,Sunit Sivasankaran,Zhuo Chen,Jinyu Li,Takuya Yoshioka
title: Simulating realistic speech overlaps improves multi-talker ASR
title_zh: 模拟真实的语音重叠可改善多人 ASR
abstract: Multi-talker automatic speech recognition (ASR) has been studied to generate transcriptions of natural conversation including overlapping speech of multiple speakers. Due to the difficulty in acquiring real conversation data with high-quality human transcriptions, a naïve simulation of multi-talker speech by randomly mixing multiple utterances was conventionally used for model training. In this work, we propose an improved technique to simulate multi-talker overlapping speech with realistic speech overlaps, where an arbitrary pattern of speech overlaps is represented by a sequence of discrete tokens. With this representation, speech overlapping patterns can be learned from real conversations based on a statistical language model, such as N-gram, which can be then used to generate multi-talker speech for training. In our experiments, multi-talker ASR models trained with the proposed method show consistent improvement on the word error rates across multiple datasets.
summary: Multi-talker automatic speech recognition (ASR) has been studied to generate transcriptions of natural conversation including overlapping speech of multiple speakers. Due to the difficulty in acquiring real conversation data with high-quality human transcriptions, a naïve simulation of multi-talker speech by randomly mixing multiple utterances was conventionally used for model training. In this work, we propose an improved technique to simulate multi-talker overlapping speech with realistic speech overlaps, where an arbitrary pattern of speech overlaps is represented by a sequence of discrete tokens. With this representation, speech overlapping patterns can be learned from real conversations based on a statistical language model, such as N-gram, which can be then used to generate multi-talker speech for training. In our experiments, multi-talker ASR models trained with the proposed method show consistent improvement on the word error rates across multiple datasets.
abstract_zh: 多说话者自动语音识别 (ASR) 已被研究用于生成自然对话的转录，包括多个说话者的重叠语音。由于很难通过高质量的人类转录来获取真实的对话数据，因此通常使用通过随机混合多个话语来简单模拟多说话者语音来进行模型训练。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种改进的技术来模拟具有真实语音重叠的多说话者重叠语音，其中语音重叠的任意模式由一系列离散标记表示。通过这种表示，可以基于统计语言模型（例如 N-gram）从真实对话中学习语音重叠模式，然后使用该模型生成多说话者语音进行训练。在我们的实验中，使用所提出的方法训练的多说话者 ASR 模型在多个数据集上显示出单词错误率的持续改善。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种模拟真实语音重叠的改进方法，以提升多说话人语音识别（ASR）的性能。
AI.motivation: 由于获取高质量真实对话转录数据困难，传统方法通过随机混合语音片段模拟多说话人场景，但无法准确反映真实语音重叠模式。
AI.method: 将任意语音重叠模式表示为离散标记序列，基于统计语言模型（如N-gram）从真实对话中学习重叠模式，并生成用于训练的多说话人语音数据。
AI.result: 实验表明，使用该方法训练的多说话人ASR模型在多个数据集上的词错误率均有所降低。
AI.conclusion: 通过模拟真实语音重叠模式生成训练数据，能有效提升多说话人ASR系统的识别准确性。

authors_text: Tuo Zhang,Tiantian Feng,Samiul Alam,Sunwoo Lee,Mi Zhang,Shrikanth S. Narayanan,Salman Avestimehr
title: FedAudio: A Federated Learning Benchmark for Audio Tasks
title_zh: FedAudio：音频任务的联合学习基准
abstract: Federated learning (FL) has gained substantial attention in recent years due to the data privacy concerns related to the pervasiveness of consumer devices that continuously collect data from users. While a number of FL benchmarks have been developed to facilitate FL research, none of them include audio data and audio-related tasks. In this paper, we fill this critical gap by introducing a new FL benchmark for audio tasks which we refer to as FedAudio. FedAudio includes four representative and commonly used audio datasets from three important audio tasks that are well aligned with FL use cases. In particular, a unique contribution of FedAudio is the introduction of data noises and label errors to the datasets to emulate challenges when deploying FL systems in real-world settings. FedAudio also includes the benchmark results of the datasets and a PyTorch library with the objective of facilitating researchers to fairly compare their algorithms. We hope FedAudio could act as a catalyst to inspire new FL research for audio tasks and thus benefit the acoustic and speech research community. The datasets and benchmark results can be accessed at https://github.com/zhang-tuo-pdf/FedAudio.
summary: Federated learning (FL) has gained substantial attention in recent years due to the data privacy concerns related to the pervasiveness of consumer devices that continuously collect data from users. While a number of FL benchmarks have been developed to facilitate FL research, none of them include audio data and audio-related tasks. In this paper, we fill this critical gap by introducing a new FL benchmark for audio tasks which we refer to as FedAudio. FedAudio includes four representative and commonly used audio datasets from three important audio tasks that are well aligned with FL use cases. In particular, a unique contribution of FedAudio is the introduction of data noises and label errors to the datasets to emulate challenges when deploying FL systems in real-world settings. FedAudio also includes the benchmark results of the datasets and a PyTorch library with the objective of facilitating researchers to fairly compare their algorithms. We hope FedAudio could act as a catalyst to inspire new FL research for audio tasks and thus benefit the acoustic and speech research community. The datasets and benchmark results can be accessed at https://github.com/zhang-tuo-pdf/FedAudio.
abstract_zh: 近年来，由于持续收集用户数据的消费设备的普遍存在，数据隐私问题引起了联邦学习（FL）的广泛关注。虽然已经开发了许多 FL 基准来促进 FL 研究，但它们都不包括音频数据和音频相关任务。在本文中，我们通过引入新的音频任务 FL 基准（我们称之为 FedAudio）来填补这一关键空白。 FedAudio 包含来自三个重要音频任务的四个代表性且常用的音频数据集，这些数据集与 FL 用例非常一致。特别是，FedAudio 的独特贡献是向数据集中引入数据噪声和标签错误，以模拟在现实环境中部署 FL 系统时面临的挑战。 FedAudio 还包括数据集和 PyTorch 库的基准结果，目的是方便研究人员公平地比较他们的算法。我们希望 FedAudio 能够充当催化剂，激发针对音频任务的新 FL 研究，从而使声学和语音研究社区受益。数据集和基准测试结果可以在 https://github.com/zhang-tuo-pdf/FedAudio 访问。

AI.motivation: 随着智能设备普及带来的数据隐私问题，联邦学习（FL）备受关注，但现有FL基准均未包含音频数据及任务，限制了相关研究的发展。

AI.result: 构建了包含噪声和标签错误的音频数据集基准，发布PyTorch工具库，为算法公平比较提供统一平台。
AI.conclusion: FedAudio为音频领域的联邦学习研究提供了标准化测试框架，有望推动声学与语音研究社区的算法创新，相关资源已开源。

search_text: FedAudio: A Federated Learning Benchmark for Audio Tasks FedAudio：音频任务的联合学习基准 Federated learning (FL) has gained substantial attention in recent years due to the data privacy concerns related to the pervasiveness of consumer devices that continuously collect data from users. While a number of FL benchmarks have been developed to facilitate FL research, none of them include audio data and audio-related tasks. In this paper, we fill this critical gap by introducing a new FL benchmark for audio tasks which we refer to as FedAudio. FedAudio includes four representative and commonly used audio datasets from three important audio tasks that are well aligned with FL use cases. In particular, a unique contribution of FedAudio is the introduction of data noises and label errors to the datasets to emulate challenges when deploying FL systems in real-world settings. FedAudio also includes the benchmark results of the datasets and a PyTorch library with the objective of facilitating researchers to fairly compare their algorithms. We hope FedAudio could act as a catalyst to inspire new FL research for audio tasks and thus benefit the acoustic and speech research community. The datasets and benchmark results can be accessed at https://github.com/zhang-tuo-pdf/FedAudio. 近年来，由于持续收集用户数据的消费设备的普遍存在，数据隐私问题引起了联邦学习（FL）的广泛关注。虽然已经开发了许多 FL 基准来促进 FL 研究，但它们都不包括音频数据和音频相关任务。在本文中，我们通过引入新的音频任务 FL 基准（我们称之为 FedAudio）来填补这一关键空白。 FedAudio 包含来自三个重要音频任务的四个代表性且常用的音频数据集，这些数据集与 FL 用例非常一致。特别是，FedAudio 的独特贡献是向数据集中引入数据噪声和标签错误，以模拟在现实环境中部署 FL 系统时面临的挑战。 FedAudio 还包括数据集和 PyTorch 库的基准结果，目的是方便研究人员公平地比较他们的算法。我们希望 FedAudio 能够充当催化剂，激发针对音频任务的新 FL 研究，从而使声学和语音研究社区受益。数据集和基准测试结果可以在 https://github.com/zhang-tuo-pdf/FedAudio 访问。 本文提出FedAudio，一个用于音频任务的联邦学习基准测试平台，填补了现有联邦学习基准缺乏音频数据的空白。 Tuo Zhang,Tiantian Feng,Samiul Alam,Sunwoo Lee,Mi Zhang,Shrikanth S. Narayanan,Salman Avestimehr cs.SD,cs.DC,eess.AS

authors_text: Kuan-Ying Lee,Yuanyi Zhong,Yu-Xiong Wang
title: Do Pre-trained Models Benefit Equally in Continual Learning?
title_zh: 预训练模型在持续学习中是否同样受益？
abstract: Existing work on continual learning (CL) is primarily devoted to developing algorithms for models trained from scratch. Despite their encouraging performance on contrived benchmarks, these algorithms show dramatic performance drops in real-world scenarios. Therefore, this paper advocates the systematic introduction of pre-training to CL, which is a general recipe for transferring knowledge to downstream tasks but is substantially missing in the CL community. Our investigation reveals the multifaceted complexity of exploiting pre-trained models for CL, along three different axes, pre-trained models, CL algorithms, and CL scenarios. Perhaps most intriguingly, improvements in CL algorithms from pre-training are very inconsistent an underperforming algorithm could become competitive and even state-of-the-art when all algorithms start from a pre-trained model. This indicates that the current paradigm, where all CL methods are compared in from-scratch training, is not well reflective of the true CL objective and desired progress. In addition, we make several other important observations, including that CL algorithms that exert less regularization benefit more from a pre-trained model; and that a stronger pre-trained model such as CLIP does not guarantee a better improvement. Based on these findings, we introduce a simple yet effective baseline that employs minimum regularization and leverages the more beneficial pre-trained model, coupled with a two-stage training pipeline. We recommend including this strong baseline in the future development of CL algorithms, due to its demonstrated state-of-the-art performance.
summary: Existing work on continual learning (CL) is primarily devoted to developing algorithms for models trained from scratch. Despite their encouraging performance on contrived benchmarks, these algorithms show dramatic performance drops in real-world scenarios. Therefore, this paper advocates the systematic introduction of pre-training to CL, which is a general recipe for transferring knowledge to downstream tasks but is substantially missing in the CL community. Our investigation reveals the multifaceted complexity of exploiting pre-trained models for CL, along three different axes, pre-trained models, CL algorithms, and CL scenarios. Perhaps most intriguingly, improvements in CL algorithms from pre-training are very inconsistent an underperforming algorithm could become competitive and even state-of-the-art when all algorithms start from a pre-trained model. This indicates that the current paradigm, where all CL methods are compared in from-scratch training, is not well reflective of the true CL objective and desired progress. In addition, we make several other important observations, including that CL algorithms that exert less regularization benefit more from a pre-trained model; and that a stronger pre-trained model such as CLIP does not guarantee a better improvement. Based on these findings, we introduce a simple yet effective baseline that employs minimum regularization and leverages the more beneficial pre-trained model, coupled with a two-stage training pipeline. We recommend including this strong baseline in the future development of CL algorithms, due to its demonstrated state-of-the-art performance.
abstract_zh: 持续学习（CL）方面的现有工作主要致力于为从头开始训练的模型开发算法。尽管这些算法在人为的基准测试中表现令人鼓舞，但在现实场景中表现出显着的性能下降。因此，本文主张在 CL 中系统地引入预训练，这是将知识转移到下游任务的通用方法，但在 CL 社区中却严重缺失。我们的调查揭示了利用 CL 预训练模型的多方面复杂性，沿着三个不同的轴、预训练模型、CL 算法和 CL 场景。也许最有趣的是，预训练对 CL 算法的改进非常不一致，当所有算法都从预训练模型开始时，表现不佳的算法可能会变得具有竞争力，甚至是最先进的。这表明当前的范式（所有 CL 方法都是在从头开始的训练中进行比较）并不能很好地反映真正的 CL 目标和期望的进展。此外，我们还进行了其他一些重要的观察，包括较少正则化的 CL 算法从预训练模型中获益更多；并且像 CLIP 这样更强大的预训练模型并不能保证更好的改进。基于这些发现，我们引入了一个简单而有效的基线，该基线采用最小正则化并利用更有益的预训练模型，再加上两阶段训练管道。我们建议将这一强大的基线纳入 CL 算法的未来开发中，因为它已展现出最先进的性能。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了预训练模型在持续学习（CL）中的应用效果，分析其在不同算法和场景下的表现差异，并提出了一种基于预训练的改进基线方法。
AI.motivation: 现有CL研究主要基于从零开始训练的模型，但实际应用中表现不佳。预训练模型虽能提升下游任务性能，但在CL领域应用不足，需系统研究其影响。
AI.method: 通过三个维度（预训练模型、CL算法、CL场景）分析预训练对CL的影响，发现改进效果不一致，并提出结合最小正则化和两阶段训练的基线方法。
AI.result: 实验表明，预训练可显著提升CL性能，但不同算法和模型的收益差异较大，所提基线方法在多个任务中达到SOTA水平。
AI.conclusion: 当前CL算法对比未考虑预训练，导致评估不准确。推荐将本文基线纳入未来CL研究，以更真实反映实际应用需求。

authors_text: Islam Debicha,Richard Bauwens,Thibault Debatty,Jean-Michel Dricot,Tayeb Kenaza,Wim Mees
title: TAD: Transfer Learning-based Multi-Adversarial Detection of Evasion Attacks against Network Intrusion Detection Systems
title_zh: TAD：基于迁移学习的网络入侵检测系统规避攻击的多对抗检测
comment: This is a preprint of an already published journal paper
comments: This is a preprint of an already published journal paper
abstract: Nowadays, intrusion detection systems based on deep learning deliver state-of-the-art performance. However, recent research has shown that specially crafted perturbations, called adversarial examples, are capable of significantly reducing the performance of these intrusion detection systems. The objective of this paper is to design an efficient transfer learning-based adversarial detector and then to assess the effectiveness of using multiple strategically placed adversarial detectors compared to a single adversarial detector for intrusion detection systems. In our experiments, we implement existing state-of-the-art models for intrusion detection. We then attack those models with a set of chosen evasion attacks. In an attempt to detect those adversarial attacks, we design and implement multiple transfer learning-based adversarial detectors, each receiving a subset of the information passed through the IDS. By combining their respective decisions, we illustrate that combining multiple detectors can further improve the detectability of adversarial traffic compared to a single detector in the case of a parallel IDS design.
summary: Nowadays, intrusion detection systems based on deep learning deliver state-of-the-art performance. However, recent research has shown that specially crafted perturbations, called adversarial examples, are capable of significantly reducing the performance of these intrusion detection systems. The objective of this paper is to design an efficient transfer learning-based adversarial detector and then to assess the effectiveness of using multiple strategically placed adversarial detectors compared to a single adversarial detector for intrusion detection systems. In our experiments, we implement existing state-of-the-art models for intrusion detection. We then attack those models with a set of chosen evasion attacks. In an attempt to detect those adversarial attacks, we design and implement multiple transfer learning-based adversarial detectors, each receiving a subset of the information passed through the IDS. By combining their respective decisions, we illustrate that combining multiple detectors can further improve the detectability of adversarial traffic compared to a single detector in the case of a parallel IDS design.
abstract_zh: 如今，基于深度学习的入侵检测系统提供了最先进的性能。然而，最近的研究表明，特制的扰动（称为对抗性示例）能够显着降低这些入侵检测系统的性能。本文的目的是设计一种高效的基于迁移学习的对抗性检测器，然后评估在入侵检测系统中使用多个策略性放置的对抗性检测器与单个对抗性检测器相比的有效性。在我们的实验中，我们实现了现有的最先进的入侵检测模型。然后，我们使用一组选定的规避攻击来攻击这些模型。为了检测这些对抗性攻击，我们设计并实现了多个基于迁移学习的对抗性检测器，每个检测器接收通过 IDS 传递的信息的子集。通过结合他们各自的决定，我们表明，在并行 IDS 设计的情况下，与单个检测器相比，组合多个检测器可以进一步提高对抗流量的可检测性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于迁移学习的多对抗检测方法TAD，用于提升网络入侵检测系统对规避攻击的检测能力。
AI.motivation: 深度学习入侵检测系统易受对抗样本攻击，现有单一检测器难以有效识别规避攻击，需探索更优检测方案。
AI.method: 设计基于迁移学习的多对抗检测器，通过并行IDS架构中多个检测器分别接收信息子集并融合决策结果进行攻击检测。
AI.result: 实验表明，多检测器组合方案相比单一检测器能显著提升对抗流量的检测准确率。
AI.conclusion: 多战略部署的迁移学习对抗检测器可有效增强入侵检测系统对规避攻击的鲁棒性。

search_text: TAD: Transfer Learning-based Multi-Adversarial Detection of Evasion Attacks against Network Intrusion Detection Systems TAD：基于迁移学习的网络入侵检测系统规避攻击的多对抗检测 Nowadays, intrusion detection systems based on deep learning deliver state-of-the-art performance. However, recent research has shown that specially crafted perturbations, called adversarial examples, are capable of significantly reducing the performance of these intrusion detection systems. The objective of this paper is to design an efficient transfer learning-based adversarial detector and then to assess the effectiveness of using multiple strategically placed adversarial detectors compared to a single adversarial detector for intrusion detection systems. In our experiments, we implement existing state-of-the-art models for intrusion detection. We then attack those models with a set of chosen evasion attacks. In an attempt to detect those adversarial attacks, we design and implement multiple transfer learning-based adversarial detectors, each receiving a subset of the information passed through the IDS. By combining their respective decisions, we illustrate that combining multiple detectors can further improve the detectability of adversarial traffic compared to a single detector in the case of a parallel IDS design. 如今，基于深度学习的入侵检测系统提供了最先进的性能。然而，最近的研究表明，特制的扰动（称为对抗性示例）能够显着降低这些入侵检测系统的性能。本文的目的是设计一种高效的基于迁移学习的对抗性检测器，然后评估在入侵检测系统中使用多个策略性放置的对抗性检测器与单个对抗性检测器相比的有效性。在我们的实验中，我们实现了现有的最先进的入侵检测模型。然后，我们使用一组选定的规避攻击来攻击这些模型。为了检测这些对抗性攻击，我们设计并实现了多个基于迁移学习的对抗性检测器，每个检测器接收通过 IDS 传递的信息的子集。通过结合他们各自的决定，我们表明，在并行 IDS 设计的情况下，与单个检测器相比，组合多个检测器可以进一步提高对抗流量的可检测性。 本文提出基于迁移学习的多对抗检测方法TAD，用于提升网络入侵检测系统对规避攻击的检测能力。 Islam Debicha,Richard Bauwens,Thibault Debatty,Jean-Michel Dricot,Tayeb Kenaza,Wim Mees cs.CR,cs.AI

authors_text: Jiale Liu,Yu-Wei Zhan,Xin Luo,Zhen-Duo Chen,Yongxin Wang,Xin-Shun Xu
title: Prototype-Based Layered Federated Cross-Modal Hashing
title_zh: 基于原型的分层联合跨模式哈希
abstract: Recently, deep cross-modal hashing has gained increasing attention. However, in many practical cases, data are distributed and cannot be collected due to privacy concerns, which greatly reduces the cross-modal hashing performance on each client. And due to the problems of statistical heterogeneity, model heterogeneity, and forcing each client to accept the same parameters, applying federated learning to cross-modal hash learning becomes very tricky. In this paper, we propose a novel method called prototype-based layered federated cross-modal hashing. Specifically, the prototype is introduced to learn the similarity between instances and classes on server, reducing the impact of statistical heterogeneity (non-IID) on different clients. And we monitor the distance between local and global prototypes to further improve the performance. To realize personalized federated learning, a hypernetwork is deployed on server to dynamically update different layers' weights of local model. Experimental results on benchmark datasets show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
summary: Recently, deep cross-modal hashing has gained increasing attention. However, in many practical cases, data are distributed and cannot be collected due to privacy concerns, which greatly reduces the cross-modal hashing performance on each client. And due to the problems of statistical heterogeneity, model heterogeneity, and forcing each client to accept the same parameters, applying federated learning to cross-modal hash learning becomes very tricky. In this paper, we propose a novel method called prototype-based layered federated cross-modal hashing. Specifically, the prototype is introduced to learn the similarity between instances and classes on server, reducing the impact of statistical heterogeneity (non-IID) on different clients. And we monitor the distance between local and global prototypes to further improve the performance. To realize personalized federated learning, a hypernetwork is deployed on server to dynamically update different layers' weights of local model. Experimental results on benchmark datasets show that our method outperforms state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 最近，深度跨模式哈希受到越来越多的关注。然而，在许多实际情况下，由于隐私问题，数据是分布式的并且无法被收集，这大大降低了每个客户端的跨模态哈希性能。并且由于统计异构性、模型异构性以及强制每个客户端接受相同参数等问题，将联邦学习应用于跨模态哈希学习变得非常棘手。在本文中，我们提出了一种称为基于原型的分层联合跨模式哈希的新颖方法。具体来说，引入原型来学习服务器上实例和类之间的相似性，减少统计异质性（非独立同分布）对不同客户端的影响。我们监控本地和全局原型之间的距离，以进一步提高性能。为了实现个性化联邦学习，在服务器上部署超网络来动态更新本地模型不同层的权重。基准数据集的实验结果表明，我们的方法优于最先进的方法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于原型的分层联邦跨模态哈希方法，解决分布式隐私数据下的跨模态哈希性能问题。
AI.motivation: 现有跨模态哈希方法在隐私数据分布场景下因统计异质性、模型异质性及参数强制一致性导致性能下降，需改进联邦学习框架以适应跨模态哈希需求。
AI.method: 引入服务器端原型学习实例与类间相似性以缓解非独立同分布影响，通过监控本地与全局原型距离优化性能，并部署超网络动态更新本地模型分层权重实现个性化联邦学习。
AI.result: 在基准数据集上实验表明，该方法优于现有最先进方法，有效提升联邦跨模态哈希性能。
AI.conclusion: 原型分层联邦框架有效解决了跨模态哈希中的数据异质性与模型个性化矛盾，为隐私保护下的多模态学习提供新思路。

authors_text: Xuanxiang Huang,Martin C. Cooper,Antonio Morgado,Jordi Planes,Joao Marques-Silva
title: Feature Necessity & Relevancy in ML Classifier Explanations
title_zh: ML 分类器解释中的特征必要性和相关性
abstract: Given a machine learning (ML) model and a prediction, explanations can be defined as sets of features which are sufficient for the prediction. In some applications, and besides asking for an explanation, it is also critical to understand whether sensitive features can occur in some explanation, or whether a non-interesting feature must occur in all explanations. This paper starts by relating such queries respectively with the problems of relevancy and necessity in logic-based abduction. The paper then proves membership and hardness results for several families of ML classifiers. Afterwards the paper proposes concrete algorithms for two classes of classifiers. The experimental results confirm the scalability of the proposed algorithms.
summary: Given a machine learning (ML) model and a prediction, explanations can be defined as sets of features which are sufficient for the prediction. In some applications, and besides asking for an explanation, it is also critical to understand whether sensitive features can occur in some explanation, or whether a non-interesting feature must occur in all explanations. This paper starts by relating such queries respectively with the problems of relevancy and necessity in logic-based abduction. The paper then proves membership and hardness results for several families of ML classifiers. Afterwards the paper proposes concrete algorithms for two classes of classifiers. The experimental results confirm the scalability of the proposed algorithms.
abstract_zh: 给定机器学习 (ML) 模型和预测，解释可以定义为足以进行预测的特征集。在某些应用中，除了要求解释之外，了解敏感特征是否可以出现在某些解释中，或者非有趣特征是否必须出现在所有解释中也很重要。本文首先将这些查询分别与基于逻辑的溯因中的相关性和必然性问题联系起来。然后，本文证明了几个 ML 分类器系列的隶属度和硬度结果。随后本文提出了两类分类器的具体算法。实验结果证实了所提出算法的可扩展性。

AI.tldr: 本文研究机器学习分类器解释中的特征必要性与相关性问题，提出算法以判断敏感特征是否可能出现在解释中或非相关特征是否必须存在。
AI.motivation: 在需确保公平性的应用场景中，需验证解释是否包含敏感特征或必须包含无关特征，以提升模型透明度和可信度。
AI.method: 将特征必要性/相关性问题映射至逻辑归纳领域，证明复杂性边界，并为两类分类器设计具体算法。
AI.result: 实验验证了所提算法在处理大规模数据时的可扩展性和有效性。
AI.conclusion: 提出的算法为分类器解释提供了理论保障与实用工具，可支持更安全的AI应用。

authors_text: Mohammad R. Rezaei,Reza Saadati Fard,Ebrahim Pourjafari,Navid Ziaei,Amir Sameizadeh,Mohammad Shafiee,Mohammad Alavinia,Mansour Abolghasemian,Nick Sajadi
title: Reverse Survival Model (RSM): A Pipeline for Explaining Predictions of Deep Survival Models
title_zh: 反向生存模型（RSM）：解释深度生存模型预测的管道
abstract: The aim of survival analysis in healthcare is to estimate the probability of occurrence of an event, such as a patient's death in an intensive care unit (ICU). Recent developments in deep neural networks (DNNs) for survival analysis show the superiority of these models in comparison with other well-known models in survival analysis applications. Ensuring the reliability and explainability of deep survival models deployed in healthcare is a necessity. Since DNN models often behave like a black box, their predictions might not be easily trusted by clinicians, especially when predictions are contrary to a physician's opinion. A deep survival model that explains and justifies its decision-making process could potentially gain the trust of clinicians. In this research, we propose the reverse survival model (RSM) framework that provides detailed insights into the decision-making process of survival models. For each patient of interest, RSM can extract similar patients from a dataset and rank them based on the most relevant features that deep survival models rely on for their predictions.
summary: The aim of survival analysis in healthcare is to estimate the probability of occurrence of an event, such as a patient's death in an intensive care unit (ICU). Recent developments in deep neural networks (DNNs) for survival analysis show the superiority of these models in comparison with other well-known models in survival analysis applications. Ensuring the reliability and explainability of deep survival models deployed in healthcare is a necessity. Since DNN models often behave like a black box, their predictions might not be easily trusted by clinicians, especially when predictions are contrary to a physician's opinion. A deep survival model that explains and justifies its decision-making process could potentially gain the trust of clinicians. In this research, we propose the reverse survival model (RSM) framework that provides detailed insights into the decision-making process of survival models. For each patient of interest, RSM can extract similar patients from a dataset and rank them based on the most relevant features that deep survival models rely on for their predictions.
abstract_zh: 医疗保健中生存分析的目的是估计事件发生的概率，例如重症监护病房 (ICU) 中患者的死亡。用于生存分析的深度神经网络（DNN）的最新发展表明，与生存分析应用中的其他知名模型相比，这些模型具有优越性。确保医疗保健中部署的深度生存模型的可靠性和可解释性是必要的。由于 DNN 模型通常表现得像黑匣子，因此它们的预测可能不容易被临床医生信任，特别是当预测与医生的意见相反时。解释并证明其决策过程合理的深度生存模型可能会赢得临床医生的信任。在这项研究中，我们提出了反向生存模型（RSM）框架，该框架提供了对生存模型决策过程的详细见解。对于每个感兴趣的患者，RSM 可以从数据集中提取相似的患者，并根据深度生存模型进行预测所依赖的最相关特征对它们进行排名。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为反向生存模型（RSM）的框架，用于解释深度生存模型的预测决策过程。
AI.motivation: 深度神经网络在生存分析中表现出色，但其黑箱特性导致临床医生难以信任预测结果，尤其当预测与医生判断相悖时，亟需提高模型的可解释性。
AI.method: RSM通过从数据集中提取相似患者并基于深度生存模型依赖的关键特征进行排序，提供预测决策的详细洞察。
AI.result: RSM能够为每个患者提供相似案例及影响预测的核心特征，增强模型预测的透明度和可信度。
AI.conclusion: RSM框架有效提升了深度生存模型的可解释性，有助于临床医生理解和信任AI预测结果。

authors_text: Sahisnu Mazumder,Bing Liu,Shuai Wang,Yingxuan Zhu,Xiaotian Yin,Lifeng Liu,Jian Li
title: Knowledge-Guided Exploration in Deep Reinforcement Learning
title_zh: 深度强化学习中的知识引导探索
comment: This paper is an extended and revised version of the work: "Action permissibility in deep reinforcement learning and application to autonomous driving", KDD'18 Deep Learning Day (2018)
comments: This paper is an extended and revised version of the work: "Action permissibility in deep reinforcement learning and application to autonomous driving", KDD'18 Deep Learning Day (2018)
abstract: This paper proposes a new method to drastically speed up deep reinforcement learning (deep RL) training for problems that have the property of state-action permissibility (SAP). Two types of permissibility are defined under SAP. The first type says that after an action $a_t$ is performed in a state $s_t$ and the agent has reached the new state $s_{t+1}$, the agent can decide whether $a_t$ is permissible or not permissible in $s_t$. The second type says that even without performing $a_t$ in $s_t$, the agent can already decide whether $a_t$ is permissible or not in $s_t$. An action is not permissible in a state if the action can never lead to an optimal solution and thus should not be tried (over and over again). We incorporate the proposed SAP property and encode action permissibility knowledge into two state-of-the-art deep RL algorithms to guide their state-action exploration together with a virtual stopping strategy. Results show that the SAP-based guidance can markedly speed up RL training.
summary: This paper proposes a new method to drastically speed up deep reinforcement learning (deep RL) training for problems that have the property of state-action permissibility (SAP). Two types of permissibility are defined under SAP. The first type says that after an action $a_t$ is performed in a state $s_t$ and the agent has reached the new state $s_{t+1}$, the agent can decide whether $a_t$ is permissible or not permissible in $s_t$. The second type says that even without performing $a_t$ in $s_t$, the agent can already decide whether $a_t$ is permissible or not in $s_t$. An action is not permissible in a state if the action can never lead to an optimal solution and thus should not be tried (over and over again). We incorporate the proposed SAP property and encode action permissibility knowledge into two state-of-the-art deep RL algorithms to guide their state-action exploration together with a virtual stopping strategy. Results show that the SAP-based guidance can markedly speed up RL training.
abstract_zh: 本文提出了一种新方法，可以大幅加快针对具有状态动作许可性 (SAP) 属性的问题的深度强化学习 (deep RL) 训练。 SAP 下定义了两种类型的许可性。第一种类型是指在状态$s_t$中执行动作$a_t$并且智能体到达新状态$s_{t+1}$后，智能体可以决定$a_t$在$s_t$中是否允许。第二种类型表示，即使不在$s_t$中执行$a_t$，代理也已经可以决定$a_t$在$s_t$中是否被允许。如果某项操作永远无法导致最佳解决方案，因此不应（一遍又一遍）尝试，则该操作在某种状态下是不允许的。我们将所提出的 SAP 属性和编码动作许可知识合并到两种最先进的深度 RL 算法中，以指导其状态动作探索和虚拟停止策略。结果表明，基于 SAP 的指导可以显着加快 RL 训练速度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于状态-动作可允许性（SAP）属性的知识引导方法，显著加速深度强化学习的训练过程。
AI.motivation: 深度强化学习在训练中常因无效动作探索浪费大量资源，而SAP属性可帮助识别并避免不可行动作，从而提升效率。
AI.method: 定义两种SAP类型，将动作可允许性知识编码至两种先进RL算法，并结合虚拟停止策略指导探索。
AI.result: 实验表明，基于SAP的指导显著缩短了RL训练时间，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: SAP属性能有效引导探索策略，为复杂问题的深度强化学习提供新思路。

authors_text: Edith Tretschk,Navami Kairanda,Mallikarjun B R,Rishabh Dabral,Adam Kortylewski,Bernhard Egger,Marc Habermann,Pascal Fua,Christian Theobalt,Vladislav Golyanik
title: State of the Art in Dense Monocular Non-Rigid 3D Reconstruction
title_zh: 最先进的密集单眼非刚性 3D 重建
comment: 36 pages, 18 figures, 3 tables; State-of-the-Art Report at EUROGRAPHICS 2023
comments: 36 pages, 18 figures, 3 tables; State-of-the-Art Report at EUROGRAPHICS 2023
abstract: 3D reconstruction of deformable (or non-rigid) scenes from a set of monocular 2D image observations is a long-standing and actively researched area of computer vision and graphics. It is an ill-posed inverse problem, since -- without additional prior assumptions -- it permits infinitely many solutions leading to accurate projection to the input 2D images. Non-rigid reconstruction is a foundational building block for downstream applications like robotics, AR/VR, or visual content creation. The key advantage of using monocular cameras is their omnipresence and availability to the end users as well as their ease of use compared to more sophisticated camera set-ups such as stereo or multi-view systems. This survey focuses on state-of-the-art methods for dense non-rigid 3D reconstruction of various deformable objects and composite scenes from monocular videos or sets of monocular views. It reviews the fundamentals of 3D reconstruction and deformation modeling from 2D image observations. We then start from general methods -- that handle arbitrary scenes and make only a few prior assumptions -- and proceed towards techniques making stronger assumptions about the observed objects and types of deformations (e.g. human faces, bodies, hands, and animals). A significant part of this STAR is also devoted to classification and a high-level comparison of the methods, as well as an overview of the datasets for training and evaluation of the discussed techniques. We conclude by discussing open challenges in the field and the social aspects associated with the usage of the reviewed methods.

abstract_zh: 从一组单眼 2D 图像观察中对可变形（或非刚性）场景进行 3D 重建是计算机视觉和图形学中一个长期存在且研究活跃的领域。这是一个不适定的反问题，因为在没有额外的先验假设的情况下，它允许无限多个解决方案，从而实现对输入二维图像的精确投影。非刚性重建是机器人、AR/VR 或视觉内容创建等下游应用的基础构建块。使用单目相机的主要优势在于，与立体或多视图系统等更复杂的相机设置相比，它们对最终用户来说无处不在、易于使用，并且易于使用。这项调查的重点是对单目视频或单目视图集的各种可变形物体和复合场景进行密集非刚性 3D 重建的最先进方法。它回顾了 2D 图像观测的 3D 重建和变形建模的基础知识。然后，我们从通用方法开始——处理任意场景并仅做出一些预先假设——并继续研究对观察到的物体和变形类型（例如人脸、身体、手和动物）做出更强假设的技术。该 STAR 的一个重要部分还致力于分类和方法的高级比较，以及用于训练和评估所讨论技术的数据集的概述。最后，我们讨论了该领域的开放挑战以及与所审查方法的使用相关的社会方面。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了从单目视频或图像中重建可变形物体和复杂场景的密集非刚性3D重建的最新方法。
AI.motivation: 非刚性3D重建是机器人、AR/VR等领域的基础技术，而单目相机因其普及性和易用性成为重要研究方向。
AI.method: 通过分类和比较不同方法，从通用场景到特定对象（如人脸、手部）逐步分析，并总结数据集与评估标准。
AI.result: 系统梳理了非刚性重建的核心技术路线，对比了不同假设下的方法优劣，并指出现有数据集和训练评估框架。
AI.conclusion: 当前方法在复杂变形建模和计算效率上仍面临挑战，需关注技术应用中的社会伦理问题。

search_text: State of the Art in Dense Monocular Non-Rigid 3D Reconstruction 最先进的密集单眼非刚性 3D 重建 3D reconstruction of deformable (or non-rigid) scenes from a set of monocular 2D image observations is a long-standing and actively researched area of computer vision and graphics. It is an ill-posed inverse problem, since -- without additional prior assumptions -- it permits infinitely many solutions leading to accurate projection to the input 2D images. Non-rigid reconstruction is a foundational building block for downstream applications like robotics, AR/VR, or visual content creation. The key advantage of using monocular cameras is their omnipresence and availability to the end users as well as their ease of use compared to more sophisticated camera set-ups such as stereo or multi-view systems. This survey focuses on state-of-the-art methods for dense non-rigid 3D reconstruction of various deformable objects and composite scenes from monocular videos or sets of monocular views. It reviews the fundamentals of 3D reconstruction and deformation modeling from 2D image observations. We then start from general methods -- that handle arbitrary scenes and make only a few prior assumptions -- and proceed towards techniques making stronger assumptions about the observed objects and types of deformations (e.g. human faces, bodies, hands, and animals). A significant part of this STAR is also devoted to classification and a high-level comparison of the methods, as well as an overview of the datasets for training and evaluation of the discussed techniques. We conclude by discussing open challenges in the field and the social aspects associated with the usage of the reviewed methods. 从一组单眼 2D 图像观察中对可变形（或非刚性）场景进行 3D 重建是计算机视觉和图形学中一个长期存在且研究活跃的领域。这是一个不适定的反问题，因为在没有额外的先验假设的情况下，它允许无限多个解决方案，从而实现对输入二维图像的精确投影。非刚性重建是机器人、AR/VR 或视觉内容创建等下游应用的基础构建块。使用单目相机的主要优势在于，与立体或多视图系统等更复杂的相机设置相比，它们对最终用户来说无处不在、易于使用，并且易于使用。这项调查的重点是对单目视频或单目视图集的各种可变形物体和复合场景进行密集非刚性 3D 重建的最先进方法。它回顾了 2D 图像观测的 3D 重建和变形建模的基础知识。然后，我们从通用方法开始——处理任意场景并仅做出一些预先假设——并继续研究对观察到的物体和变形类型（例如人脸、身体、手和动物）做出更强假设的技术。该 STAR 的一个重要部分还致力于分类和方法的高级比较，以及用于训练和评估所讨论技术的数据集的概述。最后，我们讨论了该领域的开放挑战以及与所审查方法的使用相关的社会方面。 本文综述了从单目视频或图像中重建可变形物体和复杂场景的密集非刚性3D重建的最新方法。 Edith Tretschk,Navami Kairanda,Mallikarjun B R,Rishabh Dabral,Adam Kortylewski,Bernhard Egger,Marc Habermann,Pascal Fua,Christian Theobalt,Vladislav Golyanik cs.CV,cs.GR

authors_text: Zifan Shi,Sida Peng,Yinghao Xu,Andreas Geiger,Yiyi Liao,Yujun Shen
title: Deep Generative Models on 3D Representations: A Survey
title_zh: 3D 表示的深度生成模型：调查
abstract: Generative models aim to learn the distribution of observed data by generating new instances. With the advent of neural networks, deep generative models, including variational autoencoders (VAEs), generative adversarial networks (GANs), and diffusion models (DMs), have progressed remarkably in synthesizing 2D images. Recently, researchers started to shift focus from 2D to 3D space, considering that 3D data is more closely aligned with our physical world and holds immense practical potential. However, unlike 2D images, which possess an inherent and efficient representation (\textit{i.e.}, a pixel grid), representing 3D data poses significantly greater challenges. Ideally, a robust 3D representation should be capable of accurately modeling complex shapes and appearances while being highly efficient in handling high-resolution data with high processing speeds and low memory requirements. Regrettably, existing 3D representations, such as point clouds, meshes, and neural fields, often fail to satisfy all of these requirements simultaneously. In this survey, we thoroughly review the ongoing developments of 3D generative models, including methods that employ 2D and 3D supervision. Our analysis centers on generative models, with a particular focus on the representations utilized in this context. We believe our survey will help the community to track the field's evolution and to spark innovative ideas to propel progress towards solving this challenging task.

In this survey, we thoroughly review the ongoing developments of 3D generative models, including methods that employ 2D and 3D supervision. We believe our survey will help the community to track the field's evolution and to spark innovative ideas to propel progress towards solving this challenging task.
abstract_zh: 生成模型旨在通过生成新实例来学习观察数据的分布。随着神经网络的出现，深度生成模型，包括变分自动编码器（VAE）、生成对抗网络（GAN）和扩散模型（DM），在合成 2D 图像方面取得了显着进展。最近，研究人员开始将注意力从 2D 空间转移到 3D 空间，因为考虑到 3D 数据与我们的物理世界更加紧密地结合在一起，并且具有巨大的实际潜力。然而，与拥有固有且高效的表示（\textit{即像素网格）的 2D 图像不同，表示 3D 数据提出了更大的挑战。理想情况下，强大的 3D 表示应该能够准确地建模复杂的形状和外观，同时以高处理速度和低内存需求高效处理高分辨率数据。遗憾的是，现有的 3D 表示（例如点云、网格和神经场）通常无法同时满足所有这些要求。在本次调查中，我们全面回顾了 3D 生成模型的持续发展，包括采用 2D 和 3D 监督的方法。我们的分析以生成模型为中心，特别关注在这种情况下使用的表示。我们相信我们的调查将帮助社区跟踪该领域的发展并激发创新想法，以推动解决这一具有挑战性的任务的进展。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了3D生成模型的最新发展，重点分析了不同3D表示方法的优缺点及应用。
AI.motivation: 3D数据更贴近物理世界且具实用潜力，但现有表示方法（如点云、网格等）难以同时满足高效性与精确性的需求。
AI.method: 系统回顾了基于2D/3D监督的生成模型方法，分类讨论了不同3D表示技术（如神经场、点云等）的实现方式与挑战。
AI.result: 总结了当前3D生成模型的技术路线，指出现有方法在高分辨率处理、内存效率及形状建模上的不足。
AI.conclusion: 该综述为研究者提供了领域发展脉络，并启发了未来在3D表示优化与生成算法创新方向的探索。

authors_text: Raunaq Bhirangi,Abigail DeFranco,Jacob Adkins,Carmel Majidi,Abhinav Gupta,Tess Hellebrekers,Vikash Kumar
title: All the Feels: A dexterous hand with large-area tactile sensing
title_zh: 所有的感觉：灵巧的手，具有大面积的触觉感应
abstract: High cost and lack of reliability has precluded the widespread adoption of dexterous hands in robotics. Furthermore, the lack of a viable tactile sensor capable of sensing over the entire area of the hand impedes the rich, low-level feedback that would improve learning of dexterous manipulation skills. This paper introduces an inexpensive, modular, robust, and scalable platform -- the DManus -- aimed at resolving these challenges while satisfying the large-scale data collection capabilities demanded by deep robot learning paradigms. Studies on human manipulation point to the criticality of low-level tactile feedback in performing everyday dexterous tasks. The DManus comes with ReSkin sensing on the entire surface of the palm as well as the fingertips. We demonstrate effectiveness of the fully integrated system in a tactile aware task -- bin picking and sorting. Code, documentation, design files, detailed assembly instructions, trained models, task videos, and all supplementary materials required to recreate the setup can be found on https://sites.google.com/view/roboticsbenchmarks/platforms/dmanus.
summary: High cost and lack of reliability has precluded the widespread adoption of dexterous hands in robotics. Furthermore, the lack of a viable tactile sensor capable of sensing over the entire area of the hand impedes the rich, low-level feedback that would improve learning of dexterous manipulation skills. This paper introduces an inexpensive, modular, robust, and scalable platform -- the DManus -- aimed at resolving these challenges while satisfying the large-scale data collection capabilities demanded by deep robot learning paradigms. Studies on human manipulation point to the criticality of low-level tactile feedback in performing everyday dexterous tasks. The DManus comes with ReSkin sensing on the entire surface of the palm as well as the fingertips. We demonstrate effectiveness of the fully integrated system in a tactile aware task -- bin picking and sorting. Code, documentation, design files, detailed assembly instructions, trained models, task videos, and all supplementary materials required to recreate the setup can be found on https://sites.google.com/view/roboticsbenchmarks/platforms/dmanus.
abstract_zh: 高成本和缺乏可靠性阻碍了机器人技术中灵巧手的广泛采用。此外，缺乏能够感知手的整个区域的可行的触觉传感器阻碍了丰富的、低水平的反馈，而这些反馈可以提高灵巧操作技能的学习。本文介绍了一种廉价、模块化、强大且可扩展的平台——DManus——旨在解决这些挑战，同时满足深度机器人学习范式所需的大规模数据收集能力。对人类操纵的研究表明，低水平的触觉反馈在执行日常灵巧任务中至关重要。 DManus 在手掌和指尖的整个表面上都配备了 ReSkin 传感功能。我们展示了完全集成系统在触觉感知任务（垃圾箱拣选和分类）中的有效性。代码、文档、设计文件、详细的组装说明、训练模型、任务视频以及重新创建设置所需的所有补充材料可以在 https://sites.google.com/view/roboticsbenchmarks/platforms/dmanus 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为DManus的低成本、模块化、可扩展的灵巧手平台，配备全覆盖触觉传感器，以解决机器人灵巧操作中触觉反馈不足的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有灵巧手因成本高、可靠性差及缺乏全区域触觉传感器，难以实现丰富的低级反馈，阻碍了深度学习范式下灵巧操作技能的提升。
AI.method: 开发了集成ReSkin触觉传感器的DManus系统，覆盖手掌和指尖全部表面，并通过分拣任务验证其有效性。
AI.result: 在触觉感知任务（如分拣和排序）中验证了系统的有效性，并提供了代码、模型和装配指南等资源。
AI.conclusion: DManus为机器人提供了经济高效的触觉反馈解决方案，有助于提升灵巧操作能力并推动深度学习在机器人领域的应用。

The DManus comes with ReSkin sensing on the entire surface of the palm as well as the fingertips. We demonstrate effectiveness of the fully integrated system in a tactile aware task -- bin picking and sorting. Code, documentation, design files, detailed assembly instructions, trained models, task videos, and all supplementary materials required to recreate the setup can be found on https://sites.google.com/view/roboticsbenchmarks/platforms/dmanus. 高成本和缺乏可靠性阻碍了机器人技术中灵巧手的广泛采用。此外，缺乏能够感知手的整个区域的可行的触觉传感器阻碍了丰富的、低水平的反馈，而这些反馈可以提高灵巧操作技能的学习。本文介绍了一种廉价、模块化、强大且可扩展的平台——DManus——旨在解决这些挑战，同时满足深度机器人学习范式所需的大规模数据收集能力。对人类操纵的研究表明，低水平的触觉反馈在执行日常灵巧任务中至关重要。 DManus 在手掌和指尖的整个表面上都配备了 ReSkin 传感功能。我们展示了完全集成系统在触觉感知任务（垃圾箱拣选和分类）中的有效性。代码、文档、设计文件、详细的组装说明、训练模型、任务视频以及重新创建设置所需的所有补充材料可以在 https://sites.google.com/view/roboticsbenchmarks/platforms/dmanus 上找到。 本文提出了一种名为DManus的低成本、模块化、可扩展的灵巧手平台，配备全覆盖触觉传感器，以解决机器人灵巧操作中触觉反馈不足的问题。 Raunaq Bhirangi,Abigail DeFranco,Jacob Adkins,Carmel Majidi,Abhinav Gupta,Tess Hellebrekers,Vikash Kumar cs.RO,cs.LG

authors_text: Jinus Bordbar,Mohammadreza Mohammadrezaie,Saman Ardalan,Mohammad Ebrahim Shiri
title: Detecting fake accounts through Generative Adversarial Network in online social media
title_zh: 通过在线社交媒体中的生成对抗网络检测虚假帐户
abstract: Online social media is integral to human life, facilitating messaging, information sharing, and confidential communication while preserving privacy. Platforms like Twitter, Instagram, and Facebook exemplify this phenomenon. However, users face challenges due to network anomalies, often stemming from malicious activities such as identity theft for financial gain or harm. This paper proposes a novel method using user similarity measures and the Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) algorithm to identify fake user accounts in the Twitter dataset. Despite the problem's complexity, the method achieves an AUC rate of 80\% in classifying and detecting fake accounts. Notably, the study builds on previous research, highlighting advancements and insights into the evolving landscape of anomaly detection in online social networks.
summary: Online social media is integral to human life, facilitating messaging, information sharing, and confidential communication while preserving privacy. Platforms like Twitter, Instagram, and Facebook exemplify this phenomenon. However, users face challenges due to network anomalies, often stemming from malicious activities such as identity theft for financial gain or harm. This paper proposes a novel method using user similarity measures and the Generative Adversarial Network (GAN) algorithm to identify fake user accounts in the Twitter dataset. Despite the problem's complexity, the method achieves an AUC rate of 80\% in classifying and detecting fake accounts. Notably, the study builds on previous research, highlighting advancements and insights into the evolving landscape of anomaly detection in online social networks.
abstract_zh: 在线社交媒体是人类生活不可或缺的一部分，在保护隐私的同时促进消息传递、信息共享和保密通信。 Twitter、Instagram 和 Facebook 等平台就是这种现象的例证。然而，用户面临着网络异常带来的挑战，这些异常通常源于恶意活动，例如为了经济利益或损害而盗窃身份。本文提出了一种使用用户相似性度量和生成对抗网络（GAN）算法来识别 Twitter 数据集中的虚假用户帐户的新方法。尽管问题很复杂，但该方法在分类和检测虚假账户方面实现了 80% 的 AUC 率。值得注意的是，该研究建立在之前的研究基础上，强调了对在线社交网络中异常检测不断发展的前景的进步和见解。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于生成对抗网络（GAN）和用户相似度分析的方法，用于检测社交媒体中的假账户。
AI.motivation: 社交媒体中的假账户威胁用户隐私与安全，传统检测方法难以应对复杂的恶意活动，需探索更有效的识别技术。
AI.method: 结合用户行为相似度分析与生成对抗网络（GAN）算法，通过学习真实用户特征分布来识别异常账户模式。
AI.result: 在Twitter数据集上实现假账户分类的AUC值达80%，验证了方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为社交媒体异常检测提供了新思路，同时揭示了GAN在网络安全领域的应用潜力。

authors_text: Mary Hewitt,Hamish Cunningham
title: Taxonomic Classification of IoT Smart Home Voice Control
title_zh: 物联网智能家居语音控制的分类
abstract: Voice control in the smart home is commonplace, enabling the convenient control of smart home Internet of Things hubs, gateways and devices, along with information seeking dialogues. Cloud-based voice assistants are used to facilitate the interaction, yet privacy concerns surround the cloud analysis of data. To what extent can voice control be performed using purely local computation, to ensure user data remains private? In this paper we present a taxonomy of the voice control technologies present in commercial smart home systems. We first review literature on the topic, and summarise relevant work categorising IoT devices and voice control in the home. The taxonomic classification of these entities is then presented, and we analyse our findings. Following on, we turn to academic efforts in implementing and evaluating voice-controlled smart home set-ups, and we then discuss open-source libraries and devices that are applicable to the design of a privacy-preserving voice assistant for smart homes and the IoT. Towards the end, we consider additional technologies and methods that could support a cloud-free voice assistant, and conclude the work.
summary: Voice control in the smart home is commonplace, enabling the convenient control of smart home Internet of Things hubs, gateways and devices, along with information seeking dialogues. Cloud-based voice assistants are used to facilitate the interaction, yet privacy concerns surround the cloud analysis of data. To what extent can voice control be performed using purely local computation, to ensure user data remains private? In this paper we present a taxonomy of the voice control technologies present in commercial smart home systems. We first review literature on the topic, and summarise relevant work categorising IoT devices and voice control in the home. The taxonomic classification of these entities is then presented, and we analyse our findings. Following on, we turn to academic efforts in implementing and evaluating voice-controlled smart home set-ups, and we then discuss open-source libraries and devices that are applicable to the design of a privacy-preserving voice assistant for smart homes and the IoT. Towards the end, we consider additional technologies and methods that could support a cloud-free voice assistant, and conclude the work.
abstract_zh: 语音控制在智能家居中已司空见惯，可以方便地控制智能家居物联网集线器、网关和设备，并进行信息查找对话。基于云的语音助手用于促进交互，但数据的云分析存在隐私问题。在多大程度上可以使用纯本地计算来执行语音控制，以确保用户数据的私密性？在本文中，我们提出了商业智能家居系统中语音控制技术的分类。我们首先回顾了有关该主题的文献，并总结了对物联网设备和家庭语音控制进行分类的相关工作。然后提出这些实体的分类学分类，并分析我们的发现。接下来，我们转向在实施和评估语音控制智能家居设置方面的学术努力，然后讨论适用于智能家居和物联网的隐私保护语音助手设计的开源库和设备。最后，我们考虑了可以支持无云语音助手的其他技术和方法，并结束了工作。

AI.tldr: 本文对商业智能家居系统中的语音控制技术进行分类学分类，并探讨如何通过本地计算实现隐私保护的语音控制。
AI.motivation: 云端语音助手存在隐私风险，需研究本地计算实现语音控制的可能性以保障用户数据安全。
AI.method: 通过文献综述总结物联网设备与语音控制分类，分析现有研究及开源工具，并探讨无云端语音助手的技术方案。
AI.result: 提出语音控制技术分类体系，梳理相关研究与开源资源，识别支持本地化语音助手的潜在技术路径。
AI.conclusion: 需进一步开发基于本地计算的隐私保护语音助手，结合现有开源工具与新兴技术实现云免费方案。

authors_text: Henry W. Robbins,Samuel C. Gutekunst,David B. Shmoys,David P. Williamson
title: GILP: An Interactive Tool for Visualizing the Simplex Algorithm
title_zh: GILP：用于可视化单纯形算法的交互式工具
abstract: The Simplex algorithm for solving linear programs-one of Computing in Science & Engineering's top 10 most influential algorithms of the 20th century-is an important topic in many algorithms courses. While the Simplex algorithm relies on intuitive geometric ideas, the computationally-involved mechanics of the algorithm can obfuscate a geometric understanding. In this paper, we present gilp, an easy-to-use Simplex algorithm visualization tool designed to explicitly connect the mechanical steps of the algorithm with their geometric interpretation. We provide an extensive library with example visualizations, and our tool allows an instructor to quickly produce custom interactive HTML files for students to experiment with the algorithm (without requiring students to install anything!). The tool can also be used for interactive assignments in Jupyter notebooks, and has been incorporated into a forthcoming Data Science and Decision Making interactive textbook. In this paper, we first describe how the tool fits into the existing literature on algorithm visualizations: how it was designed to facilitate student engagement and instructor adoption, and how it substantially extends existing algorithm visualization tools for Simplex. We then describe the development and usage of the tool, and report feedback from its use in a course with roughly 100 students. Student feedback was overwhelmingly positive, with students finding the tool easy to use: it effectively helped them link the algebraic and geometrical views of the Simplex algorithm and understand its nuances. Finally, gilp is open-source, includes an extension to visualizing linear programming-based branch and bound, and is readily amenable to further extensions.
summary: The Simplex algorithm for solving linear programs-one of Computing in Science & Engineering's top 10 most influential algorithms of the 20th century-is an important topic in many algorithms courses. While the Simplex algorithm relies on intuitive geometric ideas, the computationally-involved mechanics of the algorithm can obfuscate a geometric understanding. In this paper, we present gilp, an easy-to-use Simplex algorithm visualization tool designed to explicitly connect the mechanical steps of the algorithm with their geometric interpretation. We provide an extensive library with example visualizations, and our tool allows an instructor to quickly produce custom interactive HTML files for students to experiment with the algorithm (without requiring students to install anything!). The tool can also be used for interactive assignments in Jupyter notebooks, and has been incorporated into a forthcoming Data Science and Decision Making interactive textbook. In this paper, we first describe how the tool fits into the existing literature on algorithm visualizations: how it was designed to facilitate student engagement and instructor adoption, and how it substantially extends existing algorithm visualization tools for Simplex. We then describe the development and usage of the tool, and report feedback from its use in a course with roughly 100 students. Student feedback was overwhelmingly positive, with students finding the tool easy to use: it effectively helped them link the algebraic and geometrical views of the Simplex algorithm and understand its nuances. Finally, gilp is open-source, includes an extension to visualizing linear programming-based branch and bound, and is readily amenable to further extensions.
abstract_zh: 用于求解线性规划的单纯形算法是《科学与工程计算》20 世纪最有影响力的 10 个算法之一，是许多算法课程中的重要主题。虽然 Simplex 算法依赖于直观的几何思想，但该算法涉及的计算机制可能会混淆几何理解。在本文中，我们提出了 gilp，这是一种易于使用的 Simplex 算法可视化工具，旨在将算法的机械步骤与其几何解释明确地联系起来。我们提供了一个包含示例可视化的丰富库，并且我们的工具允许教师快速生成自定义交互式 HTML 文件，供学生实验算法（无需学生安装任何内容！）。该工具还可用于 Jupyter 笔记本中的交互式作业，并已被纳入即将出版的数据科学和决策交互式教科书。在本文中，我们首先描述该工具如何适应算法可视化的现有文献：它是如何设计来促进学生参与和教师采用的，以及它如何实质上扩展 Simplex 的现有算法可视化工具。然后，我们描述该工具的开发和使用，并报告在大约 100 名学生的课程中使用该工具的反馈。学生的反馈非常积极，学生发现该工具易于使用：它有效地帮助他们将 Simplex 算法的代数和几何视图联系起来并理解其细微差别。最后，gilp 是开源的，包括可视化基于线性编程的分支定界的扩展，并且很容易进行进一步的扩展。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了一种名为GILP的交互式工具，用于可视化单纯形算法，帮助学生将算法的机械步骤与几何解释联系起来。
AI.motivation: 单纯形算法是重要的教学内容，但其计算过程可能掩盖几何直观。现有工具难以有效连接代数与几何视角，需开发更直观的可视化工具以提升教学效果。
AI.method: 开发GILP工具，提供示例可视化库和生成交互式HTML文件的功能，支持Jupyter Notebook集成。通过课程实践（约100名学生）收集反馈，并开源扩展至分支定界法可视化。
AI.result: 学生反馈工具易用且有效，显著提升了对单纯形算法代数与几何关联的理解。工具已应用于即将出版的交互式教材，并支持进一步扩展。
AI.conclusion: GILP成功填补了单纯形算法可视化工具的空白，通过交互式教学增强学生理解，其开源特性与可扩展性为未来研究提供了基础。

search_text: GILP: An Interactive Tool for Visualizing the Simplex Algorithm GILP：用于可视化单纯形算法的交互式工具 The Simplex algorithm for solving linear programs-one of Computing in Science & Engineering's top 10 most influential algorithms of the 20th century-is an important topic in many algorithms courses. While the Simplex algorithm relies on intuitive geometric ideas, the computationally-involved mechanics of the algorithm can obfuscate a geometric understanding. In this paper, we present gilp, an easy-to-use Simplex algorithm visualization tool designed to explicitly connect the mechanical steps of the algorithm with their geometric interpretation. We provide an extensive library with example visualizations, and our tool allows an instructor to quickly produce custom interactive HTML files for students to experiment with the algorithm (without requiring students to install anything!). The tool can also be used for interactive assignments in Jupyter notebooks, and has been incorporated into a forthcoming Data Science and Decision Making interactive textbook. In this paper, we first describe how the tool fits into the existing literature on algorithm visualizations: how it was designed to facilitate student engagement and instructor adoption, and how it substantially extends existing algorithm visualization tools for Simplex. We then describe the development and usage of the tool, and report feedback from its use in a course with roughly 100 students. Student feedback was overwhelmingly positive, with students finding the tool easy to use: it effectively helped them link the algebraic and geometrical views of the Simplex algorithm and understand its nuances. Finally, gilp is open-source, includes an extension to visualizing linear programming-based branch and bound, and is readily amenable to further extensions. 用于求解线性规划的单纯形算法是《科学与工程计算》20 世纪最有影响力的 10 个算法之一，是许多算法课程中的重要主题。虽然 Simplex 算法依赖于直观的几何思想，但该算法涉及的计算机制可能会混淆几何理解。在本文中，我们提出了 gilp，这是一种易于使用的 Simplex 算法可视化工具，旨在将算法的机械步骤与其几何解释明确地联系起来。我们提供了一个包含示例可视化的丰富库，并且我们的工具允许教师快速生成自定义交互式 HTML 文件，供学生实验算法（无需学生安装任何内容！）。该工具还可用于 Jupyter 笔记本中的交互式作业，并已被纳入即将出版的数据科学和决策交互式教科书。在本文中，我们首先描述该工具如何适应算法可视化的现有文献：它是如何设计来促进学生参与和教师采用的，以及它如何实质上扩展 Simplex 的现有算法可视化工具。然后，我们描述该工具的开发和使用，并报告在大约 100 名学生的课程中使用该工具的反馈。学生的反馈非常积极，学生发现该工具易于使用：它有效地帮助他们将 Simplex 算法的代数和几何视图联系起来并理解其细微差别。最后，gilp 是开源的，包括可视化基于线性编程的分支定界的扩展，并且很容易进行进一步的扩展。 本文介绍了一种名为GILP的交互式工具，用于可视化单纯形算法，帮助学生将算法的机械步骤与几何解释联系起来。 Henry W. Robbins,Samuel C. Gutekunst,David B. Shmoys,David P. Williamson cs.HC,cs.GR

authors_text: Olga Vechtomova,Gaurav Sahu
title: LyricJam Sonic: A Generative System for Real-Time Composition and Musical Improvisation
title_zh: LyricJam Sonic：实时作曲和音乐即兴创作的生成系统
abstract: Electronic music artists and sound designers have unique workflow practices that necessitate specialized approaches for developing music information retrieval and creativity support tools. Furthermore, electronic music instruments, such as modular synthesizers, have near-infinite possibilities for sound creation and can be combined to create unique and complex audio paths. The process of discovering interesting sounds is often serendipitous and impossible to replicate. For this reason, many musicians in electronic genres record audio output at all times while they work in the studio. Subsequently, it is difficult for artists to rediscover audio segments that might be suitable for use in their compositions from thousands of hours of recordings. In this paper, we describe LyricJam Sonic -- a novel creative tool for musicians to rediscover their previous recordings, re-contextualize them with other recordings, and create original live music compositions in real-time. A bi-modal AI-driven approach uses generated lyric lines to find matching audio clips from the artist's past studio recordings, and uses them to generate new lyric lines, which in turn are used to find other clips, thus creating a continuous and evolving stream of music and lyrics. The intent is to keep the artists in a state of creative flow conducive to music creation rather than taking them into an analytical/critical state of deliberately searching for past audio segments. The system can run in either a fully autonomous mode without user input, or in a live performance mode, where the artist plays live music, while the system "listens" and creates a continuous stream of music and lyrics in response.

In this paper, we describe LyricJam Sonic -- a novel creative tool for musicians to rediscover their previous recordings, re-contextualize them with other recordings, and create original live music compositions in real-time. A bi-modal AI-driven approach uses generated lyric lines to find matching audio clips from the artist's past studio recordings, and uses them to generate new lyric lines, which in turn are used to find other clips, thus creating a continuous and evolving stream of music and lyrics. The intent is to keep the artists in a state of creative flow conducive to music creation rather than taking them into an analytical/critical state of deliberately searching for past audio segments. The system can run in either a fully autonomous mode without user input, or in a live performance mode, where the artist plays live music, while the system "listens" and creates a continuous stream of music and lyrics in response.
abstract_zh: 电子音乐艺术家和声音设计师拥有独特的工作流程实践，需要专门的方法来开发音乐信息检索和创造力支​​持工具。此外，电子乐器（例如模块化合成器）具有近乎无限的声音创作可能性，并且可以组合起来创建独特且复杂的音频路径。发现有趣的声音的过程通常是偶然的，并且无法复制。因此，许多电子流派的音乐家在工作室工作时始终录制音频输出。随后，艺术家很难从数千小时的录音中重新发现可能适合在其作品中使用的音频片段。在本文中，我们描述了 LyricJam Sonic——一种新颖的创作工具，音乐家可以重新发现他们以前的录音，将它们与其他录音重新结合起来，并实时创作原创的现场音乐作品。双模态人工智能驱动的方法使用生成的歌词线从艺术家过去的录音室录音中查找匹配的音频剪辑，并使用它们生成新的歌词线，而新的歌词线又用于查找其他剪辑，从而创建连续且不断发展的音乐和歌词流。目的是让艺术家保持一种有利于音乐创作的创造性流动状态，而不是让他们进入故意搜索过去音频片段的分析/批判状态。该系统可以在无需用户输入的完全自主模式下运行，也可以在现场表演模式下运行，其中艺术家演奏现场音乐，而系统“聆听”并创建连续的音乐和歌词流作为响应。

AI.tldr: 本文提出LyricJam Sonic系统，通过双模AI方法实现实时音乐创作与歌词生成，帮助电子音乐人重新发现和利用过往录音。
AI.motivation: 电子音乐创作中音频片段难以管理，艺术家需从海量录音中重新发现可用素材，但传统方法效率低下且破坏创作流程。
AI.method: 采用双模AI驱动技术，通过生成歌词匹配历史音频片段，并利用新生成歌词持续检索音频，形成实时音乐流。
AI.result: 系统可自主运行或配合现场表演，实现连续音乐与歌词生成，保持艺术家创作流畅性而非陷入分析性检索。
AI.conclusion: LyricJam Sonic有效支持电子音乐创作流程，通过自动化音频检索与生成技术提升实时创作效率与灵感延续性。

authors_text: Lili Chen,Wensheng Gan,Chien-Ming Chen
title: Towards Correlated Sequential Rules
title_zh: 走向相关的顺序规则
abstract: The goal of high-utility sequential pattern mining (HUSPM) is to efficiently discover profitable or useful sequential patterns in a large number of sequences. However, simply being aware of utility-eligible patterns is insufficient for making predictions. To compensate for this deficiency, high-utility sequential rule mining (HUSRM) is designed to explore the confidence or probability of predicting the occurrence of consequence sequential patterns based on the appearance of premise sequential patterns. It has numerous applications, such as product recommendation and weather prediction. However, the existing algorithm, known as HUSRM, is limited to extracting all eligible rules while neglecting the correlation between the generated sequential rules. To address this issue, we propose a novel algorithm called correlated high-utility sequential rule miner (CoUSR) to integrate the concept of correlation into HUSRM. The proposed algorithm requires not only that each rule be correlated but also that the patterns in the antecedent and consequent of the high-utility sequential rule be correlated. The algorithm adopts a utility-list structure to avoid multiple database scans. Additionally, several pruning strategies are used to improve the algorithm's efficiency and performance. Based on several real-world datasets, subsequent experiments demonstrated that CoUSR is effective and efficient in terms of operation time and memory consumption.
summary: The goal of high-utility sequential pattern mining (HUSPM) is to efficiently discover profitable or useful sequential patterns in a large number of sequences. However, simply being aware of utility-eligible patterns is insufficient for making predictions. To compensate for this deficiency, high-utility sequential rule mining (HUSRM) is designed to explore the confidence or probability of predicting the occurrence of consequence sequential patterns based on the appearance of premise sequential patterns. It has numerous applications, such as product recommendation and weather prediction. However, the existing algorithm, known as HUSRM, is limited to extracting all eligible rules while neglecting the correlation between the generated sequential rules. To address this issue, we propose a novel algorithm called correlated high-utility sequential rule miner (CoUSR) to integrate the concept of correlation into HUSRM. The proposed algorithm requires not only that each rule be correlated but also that the patterns in the antecedent and consequent of the high-utility sequential rule be correlated. The algorithm adopts a utility-list structure to avoid multiple database scans. Additionally, several pruning strategies are used to improve the algorithm's efficiency and performance. Based on several real-world datasets, subsequent experiments demonstrated that CoUSR is effective and efficient in terms of operation time and memory consumption.
abstract_zh: 高实用性序列模式挖掘（HUSPM）的目标是在大量序列中有效地发现有利可图或有用的序列模式。然而，仅仅了解公用事业合格模式不足以做出预测。为了弥补这一缺陷，高效用序列规则挖掘（HUSRM）被设计来探索基于前提序列模式的出现来预测结果序列模式发生的置信度或概率。它有很多应用，例如产品推荐和天气预报。然而，现有的 HUSRM 算法仅限于提取所有符合条件的规则，而忽略了生成的顺序规则之间的相关性。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了一种称为相关高效顺序规则挖掘器（CoUSR）的新算法，将相关性的概念集成到 HUSRM 中。所提出的算法不仅要求每个规则是相关的，而且还要求高效用顺序规则的前件和后件中的模式是相关的。该算法采用实用程序列表结构来避免多次数据库扫描。此外，还使用了多种修剪策略来提高算法的效率和性能。基于多个真实数据集，后续实验证明 CoUSR 在操作时间和内存消耗方面是有效且高效的。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为CoUSR的新算法，用于挖掘具有相关性的高效用序列规则，以解决现有方法忽略规则间相关性的问题。
AI.motivation: 现有高效用序列规则挖掘方法（HUSRM）仅关注规则的效用性，但忽略了生成规则之间的相关性，导致预测效果受限。
AI.method: 采用效用列表结构避免多次数据库扫描，并引入相关性约束条件及多种剪枝策略以提升算法效率。
AI.result: 基于多个真实数据集的实验表明，CoUSR在运行时间和内存消耗方面均表现出有效性和高效性。
AI.conclusion: CoUSR通过整合相关性约束显著提升了高效用序列规则挖掘的质量与效率，为实际应用提供了更优解决方案。
